---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{css}
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Orbitron:wght@400..900&display=swap');
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Exo+2:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Orbitron:wght@400..900&display=swap');

body {
  text-align: justify;
  font-family: "Exo 2", sans-serif;
  background-color: #b1b7c1;
  margin-top: 50px;
  margin-left: 80px;
  margin-right: 80px;
}

h2 {
  text-align: center;
  font-family: "Orbitron", sans-serif;
    font-weight: bold;
    font-size: 60px;
  color: #43484c;
  margin-left: 0px;
}

h3 {
  font-family: "Exo 2", sans-serif;
    font-size: 40px;
  padding-top: 40px;
  color: #43484c;
  text-decoration: underline;
  text-decoration-style: dashed;
  text-decoration-color: #218ffe;
}

p {
  font-family: "Exo 2", sans-serif;
  font-size: large;
  line-height: 1.5;
  color: #ffffff
}

li {
  font-family: "Exo 2", sans-serif;
  font-size: large;
  line-height: 1.5;
  color: #ffffff
}

```

![](https://avasiaki.github.io/stats220/overwatch_logo.png)

## Match Performance Analysis

### Understanding the Data

Overwatch is a popular first-person shooter game where players work together to defeat the opposing team and complete an objective. 

For this report, I spent a week collecting data on my own Overwatch games, recording variables such as:

- Match outcome (Win, Loss, or Draw)
- Match duration (in seconds)
- Eliminations (players I killed during the match)
- Deaths (number of times I died during the match)
- Game mode (Control, Hybrid, Push, Escort, or Flashpoint)
- Hero type (Tank, Damage, or Support)

Each row in the dataset represents one match. 

The following analysis explores patterns in my game play, by exploring how often different hero types and game modes occur, as well as summarizing my overall performance. 


Overwatch Game Modes

![](https://avasiaki.github.io/stats220/gamemode_image.png)

Overwatch Hero Types

![](https://interfaceingame.com/wp-content/uploads/overwatch-2/overwatch-2-hero-gallery-500x281.png)


### A Visual Breakdown

The following graph compares match outcomes across different hero types, showing how often each role is associated with each result.

```{r fig.width = 8, fig.height = 4}
latest_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vS_rF0OiJd8elgr13B-BHTL_zzWyY0KCt0iHhhQcDdh5ImKJ-y9Zjx9wqHxLbHlAon4AikTjzq3JfZg/pub?output=csv") %>%
  rename(timestamp = 1,
         match_duration_sec = 2,
         match_outcome = 3,
         hero_type = 4,
         game_mode = 5,
         eliminations = 6,
         deaths = 7)

ggplot(data = latest_data) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = match_outcome, fill = hero_type)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#f99e1a", "#405275", "#218ffe")) + 
  labs(title = "Match Outcome compared to Hero Type", 
       x = "Match Outcome",
       y = "Number of Matches",
       caption = "Source: Overwatch Match Performance Data")

```

The next two graphs visualize how frequently each game mode and hero type occurred in my matches.

```{r fig.width = 8, fig.height = 4}

ggplot(data = latest_data) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = game_mode),
           fill = "#405275") +
  labs(title = "Matches by Game Mode",
       x = "Game Mode",
       y = "Number of Matches",
       caption = "Source: Overwatch Match Performance Data")

```

```{r fig.width = 8, fig.height = 4}

ggplot(data = latest_data) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = hero_type),
           fill = "#f99e1a") +
  labs(title = "Matches by Hero Type",
       x = "Game Mode",
       y = "Number of Matches",
       caption = "Source: Overwatch Match Performance Data")


```

```{r}

number_of_matches <- nrow(latest_data)

mean_eliminations <- latest_data$eliminations %>%
  mean() %>%
  round()

max_deaths <- max(latest_data$deaths)

total_eliminations <- sum(latest_data$eliminations)

## I am currently taking COMPSCI 101 and learning Python, so I am familiar with using ==. == is used to compare two objects, checking whether they are equal. 

number_of_damage <- sum(latest_data$hero_type == "Damage")
number_of_tank <- sum(latest_data$hero_type == "Tank")
number_of_support <- sum(latest_data$hero_type == "Support")

most_played_hero <- max(number_of_damage, number_of_support, number_of_tank)

most_played_hero_name <- max(latest_data$hero_type)

most_common_match_outcome <- max(latest_data$match_outcome)

```

### Some Summary Stats

To provide further insight into my Overwatch perfomance, several key summary values can be analysed:

-   A total of `r number_of_matches` matches were recorded
-   The most common match outcome was `r most_common_match_outcome`
-   Damage was played `r number_of_damage` times, Tank played `r number_of_tank` times, and Support played `r number_of_support` times
-   The most played hero type was `r most_played_hero_name`
-   On average, there were `r mean_eliminations` eliminations per match, with a total of `r total_eliminations` eliminations recorded
-   Across all matches, the maximum number of deaths was `r max_deaths`


![](https://media.tenor.com/-Br_KpmvEHEAAAAM/overwatch.gif)
